commercialInventory.audienceDataPolicy: Current analytics and audience notes are shared privately during sponsorship conversations. Public pages must not invent traffic, reach, sponsor logos, or audience numbers.
fanSafeRules: Sponsors cannot claim official tournament affiliation.
Sponsors cannot use official FIFA branding, trophy likenesses, or protected marks through Matchday OS.
Sponsor placements must not block core fixtures, teams, predictions, rooms, or share-card utility.
Sponsor assets must be lightweight and locally hosted or approved before launch.
Sponsor copy cannot alter standings, Prediction Points, Fan Points, fixture data, rules, cash-prize eligibility, winner verification, or editorial conclusions.

placements.label: Matchday OS main sponsor
placements.surface: Hero, sponsor one-pager, and clearly labeled utility-side placements

placements.label: Prediction challenge sponsor
placements.surface: Challenge page, prize rules page, and free-to-enter skill challenge CTAs for the $3,000 Top 20 verified-player prize pool

placements.label: Leaderboard sponsor
placements.surface: Top 20 public tracker shell and verified Matchday Leaderboard once server scoring exists. Final cash prizes require manual winner review.

placements.label: African teams tracker sponsor
placements.surface: Team tracker support strip and room discovery modules

placements.label: Country room sponsor
placements.surface: Individual African team rooms with team-safe sponsor labeling

placements.label: Daily match card sponsor
placements.surface: Daily share-card and editorial desk card templates

placements.label: Viewing-center flyer sponsor
placements.surface: Viewing-center flyer, poster, and WhatsApp event card generator

placements.label: Newsletter/update sponsor
placements.surface: Matchday update signup and newsletter mentions

placements.label: Blog/strategy post sponsor
placements.surface: Clearly labeled sponsor-safe feature posts and strategy previews

buyerQuestions.question: Who can brands reach?
buyerQuestions.answer: African football fans, diaspora viewers, country-room followers, viewing-center operators, sports media buyers, sponsors, and brands looking for useful tournament-period utility. Current analytics are shared privately during sponsorship conversations.

buyerQuestions.question: What can be sponsored?
buyerQuestions.answer: Main hub placements, the free-to-enter $3,000 Prediction Challenge after server checks and legal review, Top 20 public tracker surfaces after verified scoring exists, team tracker strips, country rooms, daily cards, viewing-center flyers, newsletter updates, and sponsor-safe editorial features.

buyerQuestions.question: What does a pilot include?
buyerQuestions.answer: A scoped placement, sponsor-safe copy review, CTA routing, launch checklist, lightweight creative requirements, and a reporting window matched to available analytics.

buyerQuestions.question: How fast can launch happen?
buyerQuestions.answer: Simple labeled placements can be prepared quickly after sponsor copy, creative, target countries, CTA destination, legal restrictions, and reporting needs are confirmed. Prize and leaderboard sponsorships require entry locks, anti-cheat review, Top 20 verification, and rules gates first.

buyerQuestions.question: How do brands request current audience data?
buyerQuestions.answer: Use the Matchday OS sponsor enquiry route. AfroTools can share current analytics and audience notes privately so public pages do not invent traffic claims.

buyerQuestions.question: What sponsor influence is not allowed?
buyerQuestions.answer: Sponsors cannot change fixture data, standings, Prediction Points, Fan Points, cash-prize eligibility, winner verification, legal rules, editorial conclusions, or product trust copy.

teamNewsPosts.label: Call-up tracker
teamNewsPosts.title: African squad call-up tracker for the 2026 World Cup period
teamNewsPosts.dek: A source-backed desk for African fans tracking preliminary squads, final lists, group fixtures, and what still needs official confirmation.

sections.heading: What is confirmed
paragraphs: FIFA has an official squad-announcement tracker for the 2026 World Cup period. Matchday OS uses that tracker as the source-of-record for African squad cards instead of inventing player lists or treating rumours as call-ups.

The tracker matters because some teams publish preliminary lists before cutting down to the final tournament squad. A preliminary list should not be treated as the final squad until the source page says so.

sections.heading: What fans should watch
paragraphs: For African teams, the useful questions are simple: has the coach named a preliminary list, has the final list been confirmed, are major injured players included, and which group fixtures does the squad immediately prepare for?
Matchday OS keeps those answers attached to country rooms, prediction cards and fixture previews so fans can move from news to matchday action.

sections.heading: Editorial note
paragraphs: This post is a tracker, not a live rumour feed. If a federation or FIFA page changes, Matchday OS should update the card, the country room and the prediction context together.

teamNewsPosts.label: Group draw
teamNewsPosts.title: Africa group paths confirmed for the 2026 World Cup period
teamNewsPosts.dek: The final draw gives African teams their group-stage routes, opening opponents and country-room storylines.

sections.heading: Why this post matters
paragraphs: The draw turns African team tracking from a broad watchlist into a real matchday product. Every country room can now show the opponents, the next fixture and the prediction action connected to that group.
Matchday OS uses the draw to power group cards, fixture rows, share cards and the African team tracker. Scores are still pending verified final results.

sections.heading: African team paths
paragraphs: South Africa are in Group A, Morocco are in Group C, Cote d'Ivoire are in Group E, Tunisia are in Group F, Egypt are in Group G, Cabo Verde are in Group H, Senegal are in Group I, Algeria are in Group J, DR Congo are in Group K, and Ghana are in Group L.
Those paths are now usable for predictions and viewing-center planning, but no qualification claim should be made until official match results are final.

sections.heading: Matchday OS action
paragraphs: Use this post as the editorial anchor for group explorer cards, country rooms and the share-card studio. It is also the safest source-backed place to link users who ask why a team appears in a specific group.

teamNewsPosts.label: Squad news
teamNewsPosts.title: South Africa preliminary squad named by Hugo Broos
teamNewsPosts.dek: Bafana Bafana move toward Group A with a preliminary squad and a clear opening match against Mexico.

sections.heading: What is confirmed
paragraphs: FIFA reported that Hugo Broos named a preliminary South Africa squad before Bafana Bafana begin Group A against Mexico.
Because the list is preliminary, Matchday OS should treat it as a call-up stage rather than a final squad until the official final squad is confirmed.

sections.heading: Why it matters
paragraphs: South Africa open the tournament against Mexico, then continue Group A against Korea Republic and Czechia in the Matchday OS fixture data. That makes the preliminary squad especially important for early predictions and country-room discussion.
The fan angle is not just who is selected. It is whether Broos has enough balance between tournament experience, midfield control, defensive recovery and transition threat.

sections.heading: Matchday OS action
paragraphs: Pin South Africa in the African teams tracker, open the country room, and use the Mexico fixture card to build the first prediction before kickoff lock.

teamNewsPosts.label: Squad news
teamNewsPosts.title: Tunisia squad call-ups published for Group F
teamNewsPosts.dek: Tunisia move into Group F preparation with Sweden, Japan and the Netherlands waiting.

sections.heading: What is confirmed
paragraphs: FIFA published Tunisia squad context for the 2026 World Cup period, with Sabri Lamouchi leading the team toward Group F.
The group path in Matchday OS lists Sweden, Japan and the Netherlands, so Tunisia fans can already connect the call-ups to specific fixtures and prediction cards.

sections.heading: What fans should watch
paragraphs: Tunisia posts should focus on structure, midfield discipline, set pieces and how the squad handles wide rotations against different styles in the group.
If later official squad updates arrive, the post should be updated rather than adding duplicate cards.

sections.heading: Matchday OS action
paragraphs: Use the Tunisia country room for fixture order, group pressure prompts and share cards. The prediction board should keep all Group F fixtures playable while clearly locking at kickoff.

teamNewsPosts.label: Squad news
teamNewsPosts.title: Senegal provisional squad announced by Pape Thiaw
teamNewsPosts.dek: Senegal enter Group I planning with a provisional list and a final cut still to come.

sections.heading: What is confirmed
paragraphs: FIFA reported that Pape Thiaw named a provisional Senegal squad for the 2026 World Cup period.
A provisional squad is not the same as the final tournament list. Matchday OS should keep the card labelled as provisional until the source confirms the final group.

sections.heading: Why it matters
paragraphs: Senegal are placed in Group I in the Matchday OS data, with France, Iraq and Norway on the group path. That mix makes squad depth, defensive leadership and attacking availability central to prediction discussion.
The country room should focus on how Senegal balance experience with fresh legs, especially across three different group opponents.

sections.heading: Matchday OS action
paragraphs: Open the Senegal room, create a support card, and make the France fixture prediction once account-backed saving is available.

teamNewsPosts.label: Squad news
teamNewsPosts.title: Cote d'Ivoire name World Cup squad under Emerse Fae
teamNewsPosts.dek: The Elephants have a named squad and a Group E route with Ecuador, Germany and Curacao.

sections.heading: What is confirmed
paragraphs: FIFA published Cote d'Ivoire squad context under Emerse Fae for the 2026 World Cup period.
Matchday OS links that squad card to Group E, where the fixture data includes Ecuador, Germany and Curacao.

sections.heading: Why it matters
paragraphs: Cote d'Ivoire posts should be practical: squad balance, midfield power, wide runners, defensive transitions and the pressure of turning a strong list into group-stage points.
The card should not imply a result or qualification state before final scores are verified.

sections.heading: Matchday OS action
paragraphs: Use the Elephants room for fixture reminders, group-table watch, share cards and prediction prompts tied to each Group E opponent.

teamNewsPosts.label: Coach news
teamNewsPosts.title: Ghana appoint Carlos Queiroz before Group L
teamNewsPosts.dek: A coaching change gives Ghana a new tactical frame before fixtures with Panama, England and Croatia.

sections.heading: What is confirmed
paragraphs: FIFA reported Ghana appointed Carlos Queiroz before the 2026 World Cup period.
Matchday OS treats this as coach news rather than squad news, because the core change is the bench and the tactical setup around Group L.

sections.heading: Why it matters
paragraphs: Ghana face Panama, England and Croatia in the Matchday OS fixture data. Queiroz is known for tournament structure, which makes defensive shape, transition control and set-piece detail the first watch points.
The post should be updated if the federation or FIFA publishes a confirmed squad list.

sections.heading: Matchday OS action
paragraphs: Open Ghana room prompts around formation, midfield cover and how aggressive Ghana should be in the opener.

teamNewsPosts.label: Qualification news
teamNewsPosts.title: DR Congo enter Group K through play-off path
teamNewsPosts.dek: The Leopards return to the World Cup conversation with Portugal, Uzbekistan and Colombia in their group path.

sections.heading: What is confirmed
paragraphs: FIFA lists Congo DR among the 2026 World Cup teams. Matchday OS connects that official team profile to Group K fixture context.
The group path in the local data includes Portugal, Uzbekistan and Colombia, so DR Congo posts should stay focused on confirmed fixtures and team-profile context.

sections.heading: Why it matters
paragraphs: DR Congo are one of the strongest country-room storylines because the room combines return emotion, diaspora energy and a difficult group path.
The safe editorial angle is progress tracking: fixtures, squad updates when confirmed, prediction prompts and group-table movement after verified scores.

sections.heading: Matchday OS action
paragraphs: Use the DR Congo room to prepare share cards, first-match predictions and group-pressure prompts without inventing squad news.
